rationale: The cited paper studies how screen color dimensions (including value/lightness) affect perceived waiting speed and downstream evaluations, while the citing paper studies sound-frequency × color-lightness correspondence effects on attention, recall, and choice—so there is plausible topical linkage around color/lightness effects in consumer settings. However, the evidence provided is only a single DOI-level match with no citation-context text, so we cannot tell whether it is used as a core theoretical/empirical basis or just background. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
